blocks.html_en: • All cells have a molecule called ATP floating around inside, which they use for energy.

sentences.en: All cells have a molecule called ATP floating around inside, which they use for energy.
sentences.zh: 所有细胞内部都有一种名为 ATP 的分子漂浮，它们将其用作能量。

blocks.html_en: • Muscle cells have proteins in them called myosin.

sentences.en: Muscle cells have proteins in them called myosin.
sentences.zh: 肌肉细胞中含有称为肌球蛋白的蛋白质。

blocks.html_en: • When ATP bumps into myosin, the myosin breaks the ATP down into ADP. This releases energy which is physically captured by the myosin as elastic strain.

sentences.en: When ATP bumps into myosin, the myosin breaks the ATP down into ADP.
sentences.zh: 当 ATP 碰到肌球蛋白时，肌球蛋白将 ATP 分解成 ADP。

sentences.en: This releases energy which is physically captured by the myosin as elastic strain.
sentences.zh: 这会释放能量，并被肌球蛋白以弹性应变的形式物理捕获。

blocks.html_en: • When triggered by neurons, myosin releases that mechanical energy.

sentences.en: When triggered by neurons, myosin releases that mechanical energy.
sentences.zh: 当被神经元触发时，肌球蛋白会释放机械能。

blocks.html_en: • When you decide to move your arm, your brain triggers many muscle cells, carefully orchestrating the myosin twitches into large-scale movement.

sentences.en: When you decide to move your arm, your brain triggers many muscle cells, carefully orchestrating the myosin twitches into large-scale movement.
sentences.zh: 当您决定移动手臂时，您的大脑会触发许多肌肉细胞，精心协调肌球蛋白抽搐以进行大规模运动。

blocks.html_en: Now, here’s something that’s crucial for our story: Very little energy is stored as ATP. Your body contains ~100 grams of ATP, representing ~10,000 joules of energy.2 But your body at rest burns ~100 watts. So you only store enough ATP to keep yourself alive for ~100 seconds. If you sprint, you could easily burn ~3000 watts, which would use all your stored ATP in ~3 seconds.

sentences.en: Now, here’s something that’s crucial for our story: Very little energy is stored as ATP.
sentences.zh: 现在，有一点对我们的故事至关重要：很少有能量以 ATP 形式存储。

sentences.en: Your body contains ~100 grams of ATP, representing ~10,000 joules of energy.2 But your body at rest burns ~100 watts.
sentences.zh: 您的身体含有约 100 克 ATP，相当于约 10,000 焦耳的能量。2 但您的身体在休息时会燃烧约 100 瓦。

sentences.en: So you only store enough ATP to keep yourself alive for ~100 seconds.
sentences.zh: 因此，您储存的 ATP 只能维持大约 100 秒的生命。

sentences.en: If you sprint, you could easily burn ~3000 watts, which would use all your stored ATP in ~3 seconds.
sentences.zh: 如果你冲刺，你可以轻松燃烧约 3000 瓦，这将在约 3 秒内用完你存储的所有 ATP。

blocks.html_en: Through the magic of eating, you’re always making more ATP. Typically, your mitochondria recycle ~1 gram of ADP back into ATP per second, the same amount you need to stay alive.3 If you start running, your body can ramp that up to ~10 grams per second, though tricks like breathing faster and speeding up your heart.4 But it takes a minute or two for your mitochondria to really get cranking.5

sentences.en: Through the magic of eating, you’re always making more ATP.
sentences.zh: 通过饮食的魔力，您总是会产生更多 ATP。

sentences.en: Typically, your mitochondria recycle ~1 gram of ADP back into ATP per second, the same amount you need to stay alive.3 If you start running, your body can ramp that up to ~10 grams per second, though tricks like breathing faster and speeding up your heart.4 But it takes a minute or two for your mitochondria to really get cranking.5
sentences.zh: 通常情况下，您的线粒体每秒将约 1 克 ADP 回收成 ATP，这与您维持生命所需的量相同。3 如果您开始跑步，您的身体可以将其提升至每秒约 10 克，尽管需要采取呼吸加快和心跳加快等技巧。4 但您的线粒体需要一两分钟才能真正开始运转。5

blocks.html_en: So then why am I able to sprint for longer than three seconds?

sentences.en: So then why am I able to sprint for longer than three seconds?
sentences.zh: 那么为什么我能够冲刺超过三秒呢？

blocks.html_en: Because creatine acts as an additional energy reservoir, coupled to the ATP reservoir. After you eat or synthesize creatine, 60% is converted into phosphocreatine. This is done by an enzyme that grabs a creatine molecule and an ATP molecule and moves a phosphate group between them. This “charges” the creatine into phosphocreatine and “discharges” the ATP into ADP.6

sentences.en: Because creatine acts as an additional energy reservoir, coupled to the ATP reservoir.
sentences.zh: 因为肌酸充当额外的能量储存库，与 ATP 储存库耦合。

sentences.en: After you eat or synthesize creatine, 60% is converted into phosphocreatine.
sentences.zh: 当您摄入或合成肌酸后，60% 会转化为磷酸肌酸。

sentences.en: This is done by an enzyme that grabs a creatine molecule and an ATP molecule and moves a phosphate group between them.
sentences.zh: 这是通过一种酶来完成的，该酶捕获肌酸分子和 ATP 分子并在它们之间移动磷酸基团。

sentences.en: This “charges” the creatine into phosphocreatine and “discharges” the ATP into ADP.6
sentences.zh: 这会将肌酸“充电”为磷酸肌酸，并将 ATP“释放”为 ADP.6

blocks.html_en: But if your ATP levels drop—e.g. because you’re running away from a tiger—those enzymes will run in reverse, meaning they “discharge” phosphocreatine into creatine and “charge” ADP back into ATP. This happens almost instantly, so that ATP and phosphocreatine deplete at the same rate.7

sentences.en: But if your ATP levels drop—e.g. because you’re running away from a tiger—those enzymes will run in reverse, meaning they “discharge” phosphocreatine into creatine and “charge” ADP back into ATP.
sentences.zh: 但如果您的 ATP 水平下降——例如因为你正在逃离老虎，这些酶会反向运行，这意味着它们将磷酸肌酸“释放”为肌酸，并将 ADP“充电”回 ATP。

sentences.en: This happens almost instantly, so that ATP and phosphocreatine deplete at the same rate.7
sentences.zh: 这种情况几乎立即发生，因此 ATP 和磷酸肌酸以相同的速率消耗。7

blocks.html_en: At rest, your muscles contain around 3-4 times as much phosphocreatine as ATP. So the “extra” energy storage in phosphocreatine is much larger than the “base” storage in ATP itself. That’s why you can sprint for ten seconds rather than just three seconds.

sentences.en: At rest, your muscles contain around 3-4 times as much phosphocreatine as ATP.
sentences.zh: 休息时，肌肉中磷酸肌酸的含量约为 ATP 的 3-4 倍。

sentences.en: So the “extra” energy storage in phosphocreatine is much larger than the “base” storage in ATP itself.
sentences.zh: 因此，磷酸肌酸中储存的“额外”能量远大于 ATP 本身储存的“基础”能量。

sentences.en: That’s why you can sprint for ten seconds rather than just three seconds.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么你可以冲刺十秒而不是三秒。

blocks.html_en: Does supplementing creatine increase creatine levels in muscle cells?

sentences.en: Does supplementing creatine increase creatine levels in muscle cells?
sentences.zh: 补充肌酸会增加肌肉细胞中的肌酸水平吗？

blocks.html_en: Yes. Typical levels are:

sentences.en: Yes.
sentences.zh: 是的。

sentences.en: Typical levels are:
sentences.zh: 典型的水平是：

blocks.html_en: • Vegetarian: 100 mmol / kg

sentences.en: Vegetarian: 100 mmol / kg
sentences.zh: 素食：100毫摩尔/公斤

blocks.html_en: • Omnivore: 120 mmol / kg

sentences.en: Omnivore: 120 mmol / kg
sentences.zh: 杂食动物：120毫摩尔/公斤

blocks.html_en: • Someone who supplements creatine: 140 mmol / kg

sentences.en: Someone who supplements creatine: 140 mmol / kg
sentences.zh: 补充肌酸的人：140毫摩尔/公斤

blocks.html_en: So, everything seems to add up. If you supplement creatine, you increase your levels by ~16.67%, implying ~12.5% more total short-term energy storage.8 That’s in line with the 5-15% increase in strength seen in creatine trials.9 It also seems to make sense that creatine trials find little benefit for endurance exercise. If you don’t have sudden bursts of activity, a larger short-term energy reservoir won’t really help you.

sentences.en: So, everything seems to add up.
sentences.zh: 所以，一切似乎都加起来了。

sentences.en: If you supplement creatine, you increase your levels by ~16.67%, implying ~12.5% more total short-term energy storage.8 That’s in line with the 5-15% increase in strength seen in creatine trials.9 It also seems to make sense that creatine trials find little benefit for endurance exercise.
sentences.zh: 如果补充肌酸，您的水平会增加约 16.67%，这意味着短期能量储存总量增加约 12.5%。8 这与肌酸试验中看到的力量增加 5-15% 一致。9 肌酸试验发现对耐力运动几乎没有什么好处，这似乎也是有道理的。

sentences.en: If you don’t have sudden bursts of activity, a larger short-term energy reservoir won’t really help you.
sentences.zh: 如果你没有突然爆发的活动，更大的短期能量储备不会真正帮助你。

blocks.html_en: But isn’t this all very strange?

sentences.en: But isn’t this all very strange?
sentences.zh: 但这一切是不是很奇怪？

blocks.html_en: Well, I find it strange. All else equal, more strength is good. The body already knows how to make creatine. If you can just raise creatine levels and get more strength with no downsides, then shouldn’t evolution have done this already? Some variant of the Algernon argument would suggest that the fact that creatine works so well should be impossible.

sentences.en: Well, I find it strange.
sentences.zh: 嗯，我觉得很奇怪。

sentences.en: All else equal, more strength is good.
sentences.zh: 其他条件都一样，力量越大越好。

sentences.en: The body already knows how to make creatine.
sentences.zh: 身体已经知道如何产生肌酸。

sentences.en: If you can just raise creatine levels and get more strength with no downsides, then shouldn’t evolution have done this already?
sentences.zh: 如果你可以提高肌酸水平并获得更多力量而没有任何副作用，那么进化不应该已经做到了这一点吗？

sentences.en: Some variant of the Algernon argument would suggest that the fact that creatine works so well should be impossible.
sentences.zh: 阿尔吉农论点的某些变体表明，肌酸如此有效的事实应该是不可能的。

blocks.html_en: You might think that higher creatine levels are bad somehow, and that’s why evolution didn’t make them higher. But that seems wrong. Creatine levels vary naturally based on what you eat. If higher levels were bad, evolution could have brought them down. But it doesn’t. It just lets them vary.

sentences.en: You might think that higher creatine levels are bad somehow, and that’s why evolution didn’t make them higher.
sentences.zh: 您可能认为较高的肌酸水平在某种程度上是不好的，这就是为什么进化没有使肌酸水平更高。

sentences.en: But that seems wrong.
sentences.zh: 但这似乎是错误的。

sentences.en: Creatine levels vary naturally based on what you eat.
sentences.zh: 肌酸水平自然会根据您所吃的食物而变化。

sentences.en: If higher levels were bad, evolution could have brought them down.
sentences.zh: 如果更高的水平是不好的，那么进化可能会让它们下降。

sentences.en: But it doesn’t.
sentences.zh: 但事实并非如此。

sentences.en: It just lets them vary.
sentences.zh: 它只是让它们有所不同。

blocks.html_en: Often, evolution makes us “worse” to reduce our energy expenditures, because evolution hates it when we starve to death.10 But the body only spends 1-2 calories per day synthesizing creatine, and more creatine in muscle cells doesn’t have any significant metabolic cost.

sentences.en: Often, evolution makes us “worse” to reduce our energy expenditures, because evolution hates it when we starve to death.10 But the body only spends 1-2 calories per day synthesizing creatine, and more creatine in muscle cells doesn’t have any significant metabolic cost.
sentences.zh: 通常，进化会让我们“更糟糕”地减少能量消耗，因为进化讨厌我们饿死。10但身体每天只消耗 1-2 卡路里来合成肌酸，肌肉细胞中更多的肌酸不会产生任何显着的代谢成本。

blocks.html_en: I think the boring explanation is that for our evolutionary ancestors, modest increases in short-term strength just weren’t a big deal. We were exhaustion hunters, not 1-rep max deadlift hunters.11 Also, more creatine causes your muscle cells to draw in some extra water, which slightly increases energy usage for long-distance running.12 So, if you happened to get extra creatine from meat, great. If not, whatever. In the range where creatine fluctuates based on diet, I suspect creatine levels just didn’t have much impact on reproductive success.

sentences.en: I think the boring explanation is that for our evolutionary ancestors, modest increases in short-term strength just weren’t a big deal.
sentences.zh: 我认为无聊的解释是，对于我们的进化祖先来说，短期力量的适度增长并不是什么大问题。

sentences.en: We were exhaustion hunters, not 1-rep max deadlift hunters.11 Also, more creatine causes your muscle cells to draw in some extra water, which slightly increases energy usage for long-distance running.12 So, if you happened to get extra creatine from meat, great.
sentences.zh: 我们是精疲力尽的猎人，而不是最多重复 1 次硬拉的猎人。11 此外，更多的肌酸会导致您的肌肉细胞吸收一些额外的水，这会稍微增加长跑的能量消耗。12 因此，如果您碰巧从肉类中获得了额外的肌酸，那就太好了。

sentences.en: If not, whatever.
sentences.zh: 如果没有，那也没关系。

sentences.en: In the range where creatine fluctuates based on diet, I suspect creatine levels just didn’t have much impact on reproductive success.
sentences.zh: 在肌酸根据饮食波动的范围内，我怀疑肌酸水平对生殖成功没有太大影响。

blocks.html_en: Still, we must acknowledge that creatine is unusual. I wish we could tell our bodies, “Hey, we have access to unlimited amounts of food. Stop worrying about conserving energy and concentrate on being awesome.” But we have very few ways to do that. As far as I can tell, the list of normal nutrients that have been proven to increase strength is: protein, creatine, beta-alanine, the end.

sentences.en: Still, we must acknowledge that creatine is unusual.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，我们必须承认肌酸是不寻常的。

sentences.en: I wish we could tell our bodies, “Hey, we have access to unlimited amounts of food.
sentences.zh: 我希望我们能告诉我们的身体，“嘿，我们可以获得无限量的食物。

sentences.en: Stop worrying about conserving energy and concentrate on being awesome.”
sentences.zh: 别再担心节省能量，专注于变得更出色。”

sentences.en: But we have very few ways to do that.
sentences.zh: 但我们几乎没有办法做到这一点。

sentences.en: As far as I can tell, the list of normal nutrients that have been proven to increase strength is: protein, creatine, beta-alanine, the end.
sentences.zh: 据我所知，已被证明可以增加力量的正常营养素清单是：蛋白质、肌酸、β-丙氨酸，最后。

blocks.html_en: So creatine is special. And creatine makes you a little stronger. Does it make you a little smarter, too?

sentences.en: So creatine is special.
sentences.zh: 所以肌酸很特别。

sentences.en: And creatine makes you a little stronger.
sentences.zh: 肌酸可以让你变得更强壮。

sentences.en: Does it make you a little smarter, too?
sentences.zh: 它是否也让你变得更聪明一点？

blocks.html_en: Is creatine used by the brain?

sentences.en: Is creatine used by the brain?
sentences.zh: 肌酸被大脑使用吗？

blocks.html_en: Yes. Most parts of the body don’t contain significant creatine. But the brain does, along with muscles, the heart, and testes. Neurons use it to play the same game muscles do with ATP and phosphate groups and so on.

sentences.en: Yes.
sentences.zh: 是的。

sentences.en: Most parts of the body don’t contain significant creatine.
sentences.zh: 身体的大部分部位都不含有大量的肌酸。

sentences.en: But the brain does, along with muscles, the heart, and testes.
sentences.zh: 但大脑、肌肉、心脏和睾丸确实如此。

sentences.en: Neurons use it to play the same game muscles do with ATP and phosphate groups and so on.
sentences.zh: 神经元用它来玩肌肉与 ATP 和磷酸盐基团等相同的游戏。

blocks.html_en: How much creatine is in the brain?

sentences.en: How much creatine is in the brain?
sentences.zh: 大脑中有多少肌酸？

blocks.html_en: Maybe half as much as in muscle. The number of interest here is the ratio of phosphocreatine to ATP, indicating how much phosphocreatine increases local energy storage. We saw above that in muscle, that ratio is 3 to 4. In the brain, the numbers are a little sketchy, but the ratio seems to be more like 1.5 to 2.13

sentences.en: Maybe half as much as in muscle.
sentences.zh: 也许是肌肉的一半。

sentences.en: The number of interest here is the ratio of phosphocreatine to ATP, indicating how much phosphocreatine increases local energy storage.
sentences.zh: 这里感兴趣的数字是磷酸肌酸与 ATP 的比率，表明磷酸肌酸增加局部能量储存的程度。

sentences.en: We saw above that in muscle, that ratio is 3 to 4.
sentences.zh: 我们在上面看到肌肉的比例是 3 比 4。

sentences.en: In the brain, the numbers are a little sketchy, but the ratio seems to be more like 1.5 to 2.13
sentences.zh: 在大脑中，数字有点粗略，但比例似乎更像是 1.5 到 2.13

blocks.html_en: But why? Why would the brain use creatine?

sentences.en: But why?
sentences.zh: 但为什么？

sentences.en: Why would the brain use creatine?
sentences.zh: 为什么大脑会使用肌酸？

blocks.html_en: Good question! The brain doesn’t have bursts of energy usage like muscles do. Yes, the brain uses ~20% of all calories despite only making up ~2% of body mass. But the brain is unusual in that it needs all that energy just for basic housekeeping, and doesn’t ramp up with usage. Contrary to the common myth, thinking hard does not burn significantly more calories. (Demonstration: Start thinking hard, and watch as your heart rate does not increase.)

sentences.en: Good question!
sentences.zh: 好问题！

sentences.en: The brain doesn’t have bursts of energy usage like muscles do.
sentences.zh: 大脑不像肌肉那样消耗能量。

sentences.en: Yes, the brain uses ~20% of all calories despite only making up ~2% of body mass.
sentences.zh: 是的，大脑消耗了约 20% 的卡路里，尽管大脑只占体重的约 2%。

sentences.en: But the brain is unusual in that it needs all that energy just for basic housekeeping, and doesn’t ramp up with usage.
sentences.zh: 但大脑的不同寻常之处在于，它需要所有能量来完成基本的家务管理，并且不会随着使用而增加。

sentences.en: Contrary to the common myth, thinking hard does not burn significantly more calories.
sentences.zh: 与普遍的误解相反，努力思考并不会燃烧更多的卡路里。

sentences.en: (Demonstration: Start thinking hard, and watch as your heart rate does not increase.)
sentences.zh: （示范：开始认真思考，观察你的心率是否增加。）

blocks.html_en: So muscles use creatine for sprints. But the brain doesn’t have sprints. So what the hell is the brain using creatine for?

sentences.en: So muscles use creatine for sprints.
sentences.zh: 因此肌肉使用肌酸进行冲刺。

sentences.en: But the brain doesn’t have sprints.
sentences.zh: 但大脑没有冲刺。

sentences.en: So what the hell is the brain using creatine for?
sentences.zh: 那么大脑到底用肌酸做什么呢？

blocks.html_en: The most common theory seems to go like this: Actually, muscles don’t just use creatine as an extra energy reservoir. They also use it to deliver energy inside of cells. You see, creatine diffuses faster than ATP inside of cells. So even with endurance exercise, creatine is still being used: Enzymes near the mitochondria use ATP to “charge” creatine into phosphocreatine and enzymes near myosin use that phosphocreatine to “recharge” ADP back into ATP. Even though the net change in creatine is zero, it helps “shuttle” energy from the mitochondria to the myosin.

sentences.en: The most common theory seems to go like this: Actually, muscles don’t just use creatine as an extra energy reservoir.
sentences.zh: 最常见的理论似乎是这样的：实际上，肌肉不仅仅使用肌酸作为额外的能量储存库。

sentences.en: They also use it to deliver energy inside of cells.
sentences.zh: 他们还用它在细胞内传递能量。

sentences.en: You see, creatine diffuses faster than ATP inside of cells.
sentences.zh: 您会看到，肌酸在细胞内的扩散速度比 ATP 快。

sentences.en: So even with endurance exercise, creatine is still being used: Enzymes near the mitochondria use ATP to “charge” creatine into phosphocreatine and enzymes near myosin use that phosphocreatine to “recharge” ADP back into ATP.
sentences.zh: 因此，即使进行耐力运动，肌酸仍然被使用：线粒体附近的酶使用 ATP 将肌酸“充电”为磷酸肌酸，肌球蛋白附近的酶使用磷酸肌酸将 ADP“充电”回 ATP。

sentences.en: Even though the net change in creatine is zero, it helps “shuttle” energy from the mitochondria to the myosin.
sentences.zh: 尽管肌酸的净变化为零，但它有助于将能量从线粒体“穿梭”到肌球蛋白。

blocks.html_en: Under this theory, what neurons and muscle cells share is that parts of the cell locally use a lot of energy, when they get triggered. So even though your brain doesn’t “sprint”, it still uses creatine to avoid local energy deficits.

sentences.en: Under this theory, what neurons and muscle cells share is that parts of the cell locally use a lot of energy, when they get triggered.
sentences.zh: 根据这个理论，神经元和肌肉细胞的共同之处在于，当它们被触发时，细胞的某些部分会局部使用大量能量。

sentences.en: So even though your brain doesn’t “sprint”, it still uses creatine to avoid local energy deficits.
sentences.zh: 因此，即使您的大脑没有“冲刺”，它仍然会使用肌酸来避免局部能量不足。

blocks.html_en: There’s also experimental evidence that creatine is important for the brain. We’ve created genetically altered mice with brains that lack the enzymes needed to convert creatine to and from phosphocreatine. They display severely limited spatial learning and somewhat smaller brains.

sentences.en: There’s also experimental evidence that creatine is important for the brain.
sentences.zh: 还有实验证据表明肌酸对大脑很重要。

sentences.en: We’ve created genetically altered mice with brains that lack the enzymes needed to convert creatine to and from phosphocreatine.
sentences.zh: 我们培育出了经过基因改造的小鼠，其大脑缺乏将肌酸转化为磷酸肌酸或从磷酸肌酸转化为肌酸所需的酶。

sentences.en: They display severely limited spatial learning and somewhat smaller brains.
sentences.zh: 他们的空间学习能力非常有限，大脑也较小。

blocks.html_en: Some humans also naturally have creatine deficiency. In some variants, people have trouble synthesizing creatine. This leads to lower levels throughout the body, including skeletal muscle where 95% of creatine lives. Nevertheless, the primary symptom is related to the brain, namely intellectual disability. Muscle weakness and seizures are also common. Other people have creatine transporter deficiency, meaning creatine can’t cross the blood-brain barrier. This leads to lower levels in the brain only. This leads again to intellectual disability and also often muscle weakness or seizures. (That muscle weakness is despite the fact that the muscle cells themselves have normal creatine levels.)14

sentences.en: Some humans also naturally have creatine deficiency.
sentences.zh: 有些人天生就患有肌酸缺乏症。

sentences.en: In some variants, people have trouble synthesizing creatine.
sentences.zh: 在某些变体中，人们无法合成肌酸。

sentences.en: This leads to lower levels throughout the body, including skeletal muscle where 95% of creatine lives.
sentences.zh: 这会导致全身肌酸水平降低，其中包括 95% 的肌酸所在的骨骼肌。

sentences.en: Nevertheless, the primary symptom is related to the brain, namely intellectual disability.
sentences.zh: 然而，主要症状与大脑有关，即智力障碍。

sentences.en: Muscle weakness and seizures are also common.
sentences.zh: 肌肉无力和癫痫发作也很常见。

sentences.en: Other people have creatine transporter deficiency, meaning creatine can’t cross the blood-brain barrier.
sentences.zh: 其他人患有肌酸转运蛋白缺乏症，这意味着肌酸无法穿过血脑屏障。

sentences.en: This leads to lower levels in the brain only.
sentences.zh: 这只会导致大脑中的水平降低。

sentences.en: This leads again to intellectual disability and also often muscle weakness or seizures.
sentences.zh: 这再次导致智力障碍，并且常常导致肌肉无力或癫痫发作。

sentences.en: (That muscle weakness is despite the fact that the muscle cells themselves have normal creatine levels.)14
sentences.zh: （尽管肌肉细胞本身具有正常的肌酸水平，但肌肉无力仍然存在。）14

blocks.html_en: So somehow, creatine is very important for the brain.

sentences.en: So somehow, creatine is very important for the brain.
sentences.zh: 所以不知何故，肌酸对大脑非常重要。

blocks.html_en: Does supplementing creatine increase creatine levels in the brain?

sentences.en: Does supplementing creatine increase creatine levels in the brain?
sentences.zh: 补充肌酸会增加大脑中的肌酸水平吗？

blocks.html_en: Probably, though likely less than in muscle.

sentences.en: Probably, though likely less than in muscle.
sentences.zh: 有可能，尽管可能比肌肉少。

blocks.html_en: Creatine can definitely cross the blood-brain barrier. However, the protein that helps it cross is not abundant, and there are some suggestions that it’s down-regulated with prolonged creatine consumption. The brain itself can synthesize some creatine, and this too might be down-regulated by prolonged consumption.

sentences.en: Creatine can definitely cross the blood-brain barrier.
sentences.zh: 肌酸绝对可以穿过血脑屏障。

sentences.en: However, the protein that helps it cross is not abundant, and there are some suggestions that it’s down-regulated with prolonged creatine consumption.
sentences.zh: 然而，帮助其穿过的蛋白质并不丰富，并且有一些建议表明，随着长期肌酸的消耗，它的调节会下调。

sentences.en: The brain itself can synthesize some creatine, and this too might be down-regulated by prolonged consumption.
sentences.zh: 大脑本身可以合成一些肌酸，而长期食用也可能会下调肌酸的含量。

blocks.html_en: Of course, you can just give people creatine and see what happens to their brains. There have been around a dozen such studies. Most report increases between 3% and 10%, although a few report no change. However, because brains are hard to access, these studies rely on magnetic resonance spectroscopy, and some suggest that these measurements are unreliable.

sentences.en: Of course, you can just give people creatine and see what happens to their brains.
sentences.zh: 当然，你可以给人们肌酸，看看他们的大脑会发生什么变化。

sentences.en: There have been around a dozen such studies.
sentences.zh: 类似的研究大约有十几个。

sentences.en: Most report increases between 3% and 10%, although a few report no change.
sentences.zh: 大多数报告增加了 3% 到 10%，但也有少数报告没有变化。

sentences.en: However, because brains are hard to access, these studies rely on magnetic resonance spectroscopy, and some suggest that these measurements are unreliable.
sentences.zh: 然而，由于大脑很难进入，这些研究依赖于磁共振波谱学，一些人认为这些测量结果不可靠。

blocks.html_en: In people who can’t synthesize creatine, oral supplementation seems to normalize levels in the brain. (Some cognitive impairment usually remains. One patient was diagnosed and began supplementing at three weeks of age and had no intellectual disability.) So supplementing can increase brain levels in some circumstances.

sentences.en: In people who can’t synthesize creatine, oral supplementation seems to normalize levels in the brain.
sentences.zh: 对于无法合成肌酸的人来说，口服补充剂似乎可以使大脑中的肌酸水平正常化。

sentences.en: (Some cognitive impairment usually remains.
sentences.zh: （通常仍然存在一些认知障碍。

sentences.en: One patient was diagnosed and began supplementing at three weeks of age and had no intellectual disability.)
sentences.zh: 一名患者在三周大时被诊断并开始补充，没有智力障碍。）

sentences.en: So supplementing can increase brain levels in some circumstances.
sentences.zh: 因此，在某些情况下，补充可以提高大脑水平。

blocks.html_en: My best guess is that supplementing does usually increase levels in the brain, and that an increase of 3% to 10% is plausible. But the evidence isn’t particularly strong.

sentences.en: My best guess is that supplementing does usually increase levels in the brain, and that an increase of 3% to 10% is plausible.
sentences.zh: 我最好的猜测是，补充剂通常确实会增加大脑中的水平，并且增加 3% 到 10% 是合理的。

sentences.en: But the evidence isn’t particularly strong.
sentences.zh: 但证据并不是特别有力。

blocks.html_en: Why did people get interested in creatine having cognitive benefits?

sentences.en: Why did people get interested in creatine having cognitive benefits?
sentences.zh: 为什么人们对具有认知益处的肌酸感兴趣？

blocks.html_en: Because of Rae et al. (2003). They took a group of 45 healthy vegetarian or vegan university students in Australia. They did a cross-over trial where half of people got 5 grams of creatine per day for six weeks, followed by a six-week wash-out period, followed by the other half of people getting creatine. Their results were amazing, with huge improvements on Raven’s matrices (RAPM) and backward digit span (BDS):

sentences.en: Because of Rae et al. (2003).
sentences.zh: 因为 Rae 等人。 （2003）。

sentences.en: They took a group of 45 healthy vegetarian or vegan university students in Australia.
sentences.zh: 他们带了 45 名健康的素食主义者或澳大利亚纯素大学学生。

sentences.en: They did a cross-over trial where half of people got 5 grams of creatine per day for six weeks, followed by a six-week wash-out period, followed by the other half of people getting creatine.
sentences.zh: 他们进行了一项交叉试验，其中一半的人每天摄入 5 克肌酸，持续六周，然后是六周的清除期，然后另一半人摄入肌酸。

sentences.en: Their results were amazing, with huge improvements on Raven’s matrices (RAPM) and backward digit span (BDS):
sentences.zh: 他们的结果令人惊叹，对 Raven 矩阵 (RAPM) 和后向数字跨度 (BDS) 有了巨大的改进：

blocks.html_en: In their analysis, creatine increased BDS by 1.19 standard deviations, and RAPM by 1.76 standard deviations. If we convert those numbers to IQ points (where 1 standard deviation ←> 15 IQ points), that would mean increases of 17.85 and 26.4 IQ points, respectively. In both cases, the results were highly significant (p < 0.0001).

sentences.en: In their analysis, creatine increased BDS by 1.19 standard deviations, and RAPM by 1.76 standard deviations.
sentences.zh: 在他们的分析中，肌酸使 BDS 增加了 1.19 个标准差，使 RAPM 增加了 1.76 个标准差。

sentences.en: If we convert those numbers to IQ points (where 1 standard deviation ←> 15 IQ points), that would mean increases of 17.85 and 26.4 IQ points, respectively.
sentences.zh: 如果我们将这些数字转换为智商点（其中 1 个标准差←> 15 智商点），则意味着智商分别增加 17.85 点和 26.4 点。

sentences.en: In both cases, the results were highly significant (p < 0.0001).
sentences.zh: 在这两种情况下，结果均非常显着 (p < 0.0001)。

blocks.html_en: Does that replicate?

sentences.en: Does that replicate?
sentences.zh: 这能复制吗？

blocks.html_en: No. Following that paper various groups tried similar experiments but no one found such a large or statistically significant effect. After twenty years of inconclusive results, Sandkühler et al. (2023) set out to give a definitive reproduction. In my view, this is the highest-quality RCT ever done on the cognitive benefits of creatine.15 They largely borrowed the experimental design of Rae et al., although they did the experiment in Germany, used a larger sample of 123 people, used half non-vegetarians, and they dropped the wash-out period. Here are their main results:

sentences.en: No. Following that paper various groups tried similar experiments but no one found such a large or statistically significant effect.
sentences.zh: 不。在那篇论文之后，各个小组尝试了类似的实验，但没有人发现如此大或具有统计显着性的效果。

sentences.en: After twenty years of inconclusive results, Sandkühler et al. (2023) set out to give a definitive reproduction.
sentences.zh: 经过二十年的不确定结果后，Sandkühler 等人。 （2023）着手给出最终的复制品。

sentences.en: In my view, this is the highest-quality RCT ever done on the cognitive benefits of creatine.15 They largely borrowed the experimental design of Rae et al., although they did the experiment in Germany, used a larger sample of 123 people, used half non-vegetarians, and they dropped the wash-out period.
sentences.zh: 在我看来，这是有史以来关于肌酸认知益处的最高质量的随机对照试验。 15 他们很大程度上借鉴了 Rae 等人的实验设计，尽管他们在德国进行了实验，使用了 123 人的更大样本，使用了一半非素食者，并且放弃了洗脱期。

sentences.en: Here are their main results:
sentences.zh: 以下是他们的主要结果：

blocks.html_en: (T1 shows test results at baseline. T2 shows results after six weeks of creatine or placebo. T3 shows the results after another six weeks, where the placebo group crossed over to creatine and vise versa.)

sentences.en: (T1 shows test results at baseline.
sentences.zh: （T1 显示基线测试结果。

sentences.en: T2 shows results after six weeks of creatine or placebo.
sentences.zh: T2 显示服用肌酸或安慰剂六周后的结果。

sentences.en: T3 shows the results after another six weeks, where the placebo group crossed over to creatine and vise versa.)
sentences.zh: T3 显示了另外六周后的结果，其中安慰剂组交叉到肌酸组，反之亦然。）

blocks.html_en: Overall, everyone got better over time, probably from practice. On backwards digit span, during the first six weeks, the group getting placebo actually improved slightly faster than the group getting creatine. But when those groups switched between getting placebo and creatine, that (formerly placebo, now creatine) group improved even faster. Just staring at the graph, this suggests some benefit. On Raven’s matrices, the same thing happened, but with a greatly reduced magnitude.

sentences.en: Overall, everyone got better over time, probably from practice.
sentences.zh: 总的来说，随着时间的推移，每个人都变得更好，可能是通过练习。

sentences.en: On backwards digit span, during the first six weeks, the group getting placebo actually improved slightly faster than the group getting creatine.
sentences.zh: 从向后的数字跨度来看，在前六周内，服用安慰剂的组实际上比服用肌酸的组改善得稍快。

sentences.en: But when those groups switched between getting placebo and creatine, that (formerly placebo, now creatine) group improved even faster.
sentences.zh: 但当这些组在服用安慰剂和肌酸之间切换时，该组（以前是安慰剂，现在是肌酸）改善得更快。

sentences.en: Just staring at the graph, this suggests some benefit.
sentences.zh: 只要看一下图表，就会发现这有一些好处。

sentences.en: On Raven’s matrices, the same thing happened, but with a greatly reduced magnitude.
sentences.zh: 在瑞文矩阵上，同样的事情发生了，但幅度大大降低了。

blocks.html_en: They fit a statistical model and report an effect size of 0.17 standard deviations for backwards digit span (~2.5 IQ points, not quite statistically significant) and 0.09 standard deviations for Raven’s matrices (~1 IQ point, not even close to significant). They found no extra benefit for vegetarians, not even a non-significant benefit.

sentences.en: They fit a statistical model and report an effect size of 0.17 standard deviations for backwards digit span (~2.5 IQ points, not quite statistically significant) and 0.09 standard deviations for Raven’s matrices (~1 IQ point, not even close to significant).
sentences.zh: 他们拟合了一个统计模型，并报告向后数字跨度的效应大小为 0.17 个标准差（约 2.5 个 IQ 点，在统计上不显着），Raven 矩阵的效应大小为 0.09 个标准差（约 1 个 IQ 点，甚至不显着）。

sentences.en: They found no extra benefit for vegetarians, not even a non-significant benefit.
sentences.zh: 他们没有发现素食者有任何额外的好处，甚至没有一个不显着的好处。

blocks.html_en: As far as I can tell, this discrepancy has never been convincingly explained. Rae et al.’s 2003 experiment seems well done. The results are too large to be explained by p-hacking and too statistically significant to be explained by random noise. Maybe for some reason, Rae et al.’s cohort had lower baseline creatine levels? It’s very odd. But history suggests that when an exciting result is followed by a disappointing replication, we should bet on the disappointing replication.

sentences.en: As far as I can tell, this discrepancy has never been convincingly explained.
sentences.zh: 据我所知，这种差异从未得到令人信服的解释。

sentences.en: Rae et al.’s 2003 experiment seems well done.
sentences.zh: Rae 等人 2003 年的实验似乎做得很好。

sentences.en: The results are too large to be explained by p-hacking and too statistically significant to be explained by random noise.
sentences.zh: 结果太大，无法用 p-hacking 来解释，而且统计意义太大，无法用随机噪声来解释。

sentences.en: Maybe for some reason, Rae et al.’s cohort had lower baseline creatine levels?
sentences.zh: 也许出于某种原因，Rae 等人的队列基线肌酸水平较低？

sentences.en: It’s very odd.
sentences.zh: 这很奇怪。

sentences.en: But history suggests that when an exciting result is followed by a disappointing replication, we should bet on the disappointing replication.
sentences.zh: 但历史表明，当令人兴奋的结果伴随着令人失望的复制时，我们应该押注于令人失望的复制。

blocks.html_en: What about all the other RCTs? Doesn’t this call for a meta-analysis?

sentences.en: What about all the other RCTs?
sentences.zh: 其他所有随机对照试验又如何呢？

sentences.en: Doesn’t this call for a meta-analysis?
sentences.zh: 这难道不需要荟萃分析吗？

blocks.html_en: In principle, yes. The trouble is, most of the studies don’t report the numbers needed for a good meta-analysis. They do some experiment giving creatine to half of people and placebo to the other half, and measure how those groups do on some cognitive test. Then they fit some statistical model and report p-values or whatever. But they never actually publish the raw means and standard deviations.16

sentences.en: In principle, yes.
sentences.zh: 原则上是的。

sentences.en: The trouble is, most of the studies don’t report the numbers needed for a good meta-analysis.
sentences.zh: 问题是，大多数研究没有报告良好荟萃分析所需的数据。

sentences.en: They do some experiment giving creatine to half of people and placebo to the other half, and measure how those groups do on some cognitive test.
sentences.zh: 他们做了一些实验，让一半的人服用肌酸，另一半则服用安慰剂，并测量这些群体在认知测试中的表现。

sentences.en: Then they fit some statistical model and report p-values or whatever.
sentences.zh: 然后他们拟合一些统计模型并报告 p 值或其他什么。

sentences.en: But they never actually publish the raw means and standard deviations.16
sentences.zh: 但他们从未真正公布原始平均值和标准差。 16

blocks.html_en: Fortunately for us, Xu et al. (2024) contacted the authors for all those trials and got their raw data. According to their meta-analysis, creatine had the following effects.

sentences.en: Fortunately for us, Xu et al. (2024) contacted the authors for all those trials and got their raw data.
sentences.zh: 对我们来说幸运的是，徐等人。 （2024）联系了所有这些试验的作者并获取了他们的原始数据。

sentences.en: According to their meta-analysis, creatine had the following effects.
sentences.zh: 根据他们的荟萃分析，肌酸具有以下作用。

sentences.en: Effect size (standard deviations)
sentences.zh: 效应大小（标准差）

blocks.html_en: Overall cognitive function

sentences.en: Overall cognitive function
sentences.zh: 整体认知功能

blocks.html_en: Executive function

sentences.en: Executive function
sentences.zh: 执行职能

blocks.html_en: Attention

sentences.en: Attention
sentences.zh: 注意力

blocks.html_en: Memory

sentences.en: Memory
sentences.zh: 记忆

sentences.en: Processing speed
sentences.zh: 处理速度

blocks.html_en: Unfortunately for us, that paper is bad. They claim that several of these results are statistically significant, but a 2026 commentary points out that they made an error that amounts to double-counting the same data for several studies.17 For that reason, I haven’t shown their (incorrect) confidence intervals. If computed correctly, I suspect none of the results would be statistically significant. Technically, the above point estimates are also wrong, although the error shouldn’t systematically bias them in either direction.

sentences.en: Unfortunately for us, that paper is bad.
sentences.zh: 对我们来说不幸的是，那张纸很糟糕。

sentences.en: They claim that several of these results are statistically significant, but a 2026 commentary points out that they made an error that amounts to double-counting the same data for several studies.17 For that reason, I haven’t shown their (incorrect) confidence intervals.
sentences.zh: 他们声称其中一些结果具有统计显着性，但 2026 年的评论指出，他们犯了一个错误，相当于对多项研究的相同数据进行了重复计算。 17 因此，我没有显示他们的（不正确的）置信区间。

sentences.en: If computed correctly, I suspect none of the results would be statistically significant.
sentences.zh: 如果计算正确，我怀疑所有结果都不会具有统计显着性。

sentences.en: Technically, the above point estimates are also wrong, although the error shouldn’t systematically bias them in either direction.
sentences.zh: 从技术上讲，上述点估计也是错误的，尽管该错误不应系统性地使它们偏向任何一个方向。

blocks.html_en: In general, I have to tell you that I really don’t trust this paper. It’s very sloppy with tons of missing details. But as far as I can tell, no one else has ever assembled the data needed to do a good meta-analysis. So I think those numbers are the best summary we have.

sentences.en: In general, I have to tell you that I really don’t trust this paper.
sentences.zh: 总的来说，我必须告诉你，我真的不相信这篇论文。

sentences.en: It’s very sloppy with tons of missing details.
sentences.zh: 它非常草率，缺少大量细节。

sentences.en: But as far as I can tell, no one else has ever assembled the data needed to do a good meta-analysis.
sentences.zh: 但据我所知，没有人收集过进行良好荟萃分析所需的数据。

sentences.en: So I think those numbers are the best summary we have.
sentences.zh: 所以我认为这些数字是我们拥有的最好的总结。

blocks.html_en: So who can we trust?

sentences.en: So who can we trust?
sentences.zh: 那么我们可以信任谁呢？

blocks.html_en: I’ll tell you who I trust: The European Food and Safety Authority (EFSA). In 2024, a firm selling creatine applied to the EU to be allowed to advertise cognitive benefits. This led the EFSA to publish Creatine and improvement in cognitive function: Evaluation of a health claim pursuant to article 13(5) of regulation (EC) No 1924/2006.

sentences.en: I’ll tell you who I trust: The European Food and Safety Authority (EFSA).
sentences.zh: 我会告诉你我信任谁：欧洲食品和安全局（EFSA）。

sentences.en: In 2024, a firm selling creatine applied to the EU to be allowed to advertise cognitive benefits.
sentences.zh: 2024 年，一家销售肌酸的公司向欧盟申请允许宣传认知益处。

sentences.en: This led the EFSA to publish Creatine and improvement in cognitive function: Evaluation of a health claim pursuant to article 13(5) of regulation (EC) No 1924/2006.
sentences.zh: 这导致 EFSA 发布了肌酸和认知功能的改善：根据法规 (EC) No 1924/2006 第 13(5) 条对健康声明进行评估。

blocks.html_en: Here’s what they have to say (I’ve cut references for readability):

sentences.en: Here’s what they have to say (I’ve cut references for readability):
sentences.zh: 以下是他们不得不说的内容（为了便于阅读，我删除了参考文献）：

blocks.html_en: The Panel considers that, overall, the 10 human intervention studies […] do not show a consistent effect of creatine supplementation on cognitive function. The Panel notes that the acute effect of creatine on working memory reported in some studies […] was not observed at lower creatine doses […] or with continuous consumption of creatine. The Panel also notes that the effect of creatine […] reported in one study is an isolated finding across the body of evidence, where no effect of creatine supplementation was observed on other cognitive domains, including different facets of memory (episodic, short‐term, visual), verbal fluency, attention, alertness, processing speed, psychomotor speed, executive function and general cognitive ability/flexibility and fluid intelligence. Finally, the Panel notes that the three intervention studies conducted in diseased individuals do not support an effect of creatine supplementation on cognition.

sentences.zh: 小组认为，总体而言，10 项人类干预研究 [...]

sentences.zh: 没有显示出肌酸补充剂对认知功能的一致影响。

sentences.zh: 专家小组指出，一些研究报告了肌酸对工作记忆的急性影响[…]

sentences.zh: 在较低肌酸剂量下没有观察到[…]

sentences.zh: 或持续摄入肌酸。

sentences.en: The Panel also notes that the effect of creatine […]
sentences.zh: 专家小组还指出，肌酸的作用 [...]

sentences.en: reported in one study is an isolated finding across the body of evidence, where no effect of creatine supplementation was observed on other cognitive domains, including different facets of memory (episodic, short‐term, visual), verbal fluency, attention, alertness, processing speed, psychomotor speed, executive function and general cognitive ability/flexibility and fluid intelligence.
sentences.zh: 一项研究报告的证据是一个孤立的发现，其中没有观察到肌酸补充剂对其他认知领域的影响，包括记忆的不同方面（情景记忆、短期记忆、视觉记忆）、语言流畅性、注意力、警觉性、处理速度、精神运动速度、执行功能和一般认知能力/灵活性和流体智力。

Finally, the Panel notes that the three intervention studies conducted in diseased individuals do not support an effect of creatine supplementation on cognition.

blocks.html_en: On average, the RCTs do find a small positive effect, just not a statistically significant positive effect. As I so often point out, that’s exactly what we would expect if the true effect were positive but small. But it’s also entirely possible that this is due to random chance or p-hacking or publication bias. Gwern contacted one author and found that publication bias did in fact occur.

sentences.en: On average, the RCTs do find a small positive effect, just not a statistically significant positive effect.
sentences.zh: 平均而言，随机对照试验确实发现了微小的积极作用，但不是统计上显着的积极作用。

sentences.en: As I so often point out, that’s exactly what we would expect if the true effect were positive but small.
sentences.zh: 正如我经常指出的那样，如果真正的影响是积极的但很小，这正是我们所期望的。

sentences.en: But it’s also entirely possible that this is due to random chance or p-hacking or publication bias.
sentences.zh: 但这也完全有可能是由于随机机会、p-hacking 或出版偏见造成的。

sentences.en: Gwern contacted one author and found that publication bias did in fact occur.
sentences.zh: 格温联系了一位作者，发现确实存在发表偏见。

blocks.html_en: Overall, I think the RCTs provide very weak evidence in favor of a small benefit for healthy adults. (Perhaps 0.1 to 0.3 standard deviations, depending on the measure.) I also think they provide moderate evidence against a larger effect for healthy adults (above, say, 0.5 standard deviations) and weak evidence for a small benefit for adults that are “stressed” in some way that might diminish creatine, such as being older, vegan, or physically exhausted.

sentences.en: I also think they provide moderate evidence against a larger effect for healthy adults (above, say, 0.5 standard deviations) and weak evidence for a small benefit for adults that are “stressed” in some way that might diminish creatine, such as being older, vegan, or physically exhausted.
sentences.zh: 我还认为，它们提供了适度的证据，证明对健康成年人有更大的影响（例如，0.5 个标准差以上），并提供了微弱的证据，证明对因某种方式“压力”而可能减少肌酸的成年人有微小的好处，例如年龄较大、素食主义者或身体疲惫。

blocks.html_en: I would love to do that:

sentences.en: I would love to do that:

blocks.html_en: • Creatine is special. Very few nutrients really make you stronger, but creatine does.

sentences.en: Creatine is special.
sentences.zh: 肌酸很特别。

sentences.zh: 很少有营养素能真正让你变得更强壮，但肌酸却可以。

blocks.html_en: • Creatine is vital for the brain to function correctly.

sentences.en: Creatine is vital for the brain to function correctly.
sentences.zh: 肌酸对于大脑的正常运作至关重要。

blocks.html_en: • Supplementing creatine probably increases creatine levels in the brain, at least a little.

blocks.html_en: Yes:
• We don’t fully understand how the brain uses creatine. There is no clear mechanistic story for why supplementing creatine should make you smarter.

sentences.en: We don’t fully understand how the brain uses creatine.
sentences.zh: 我们并不完全了解大脑如何使用肌酸。

sentences.en: There is no clear mechanistic story for why supplementing creatine should make you smarter.
sentences.zh: 目前还没有明确的机制来解释为什么补充肌酸会让你变得更聪明。

blocks.html_en: • The best analogy for how the brain uses creatine is how your muscles use creatine for endurance exercise. But creatine has little benefit for endurance exercise.

sentences.en: But creatine has little benefit for endurance exercise.
sentences.zh: 但肌酸对于耐力运动几乎没有什么好处。

blocks.html_en: • It hasn’t been firmly established how much (or if) supplementing creatine increases creatine levels in the brain.

sentences.zh: 目前尚未确定补充肌酸会在多大程度上（或是否）增加大脑中的肌酸水平。

blocks.html_en: Does creatine make you smarter?

sentences.en: Does creatine make you smarter?
sentences.zh: 肌酸会让你更聪明吗？

blocks.html_en: I don’t know. Maybe a little.

blocks.html_en: You could make an argument like this: Creatine is crucial for the brain (somehow) so it’s safest to keep levels high, just in case. But I’m not sure I buy that. Creatine is crucial for the brain but there are several hints that evolution knows that, and so regulates levels in the brain more tightly than in muscles.

blocks.html_en: I might buy that argument for vegetarians or vegans. But there is scant experimental evidence for extra cognitive benefits in those groups, and even some evidence that vegetarians may not have much lower brain creatine levels, despite vastly lower consumption.

blocks.html_en: And if creatine is helpful, the likely benefit is probably quite small. Say you think there’s a 50% chance creatine increases IQ by 1 point and a 50% chance it’s useless. Is it actually worth the trouble of taking 5 grams of creatine every day for an expected increase of 0.5 IQ points? I’m not sure.

blocks.html_en: • Why do you breathe? You breathe because your mitochondria need oxygen to make ATP. When you exercise, you breathe faster so that your mitochondria can make more ATP. You can actually calculate how much ATP your mitochondria make using your VO₂ max score: For each liter of oxygen you use, you make ~21,000 joules of energy, corresponding to ~210 grams of ATP. If you have a typical VO₂ max score of 40 mL/kg/min and you weigh 70 kg, that means you are using 2.8 liters of oxygen per minute, which corresponds to ~588 grams of ATP per minute or ~9.8 grams per second. ↩

blocks.html_en:
• There’s also stored energy in the form of glycogen. This takes a few seconds to come online, and lasts a few minutes. In a sprint, you aren’t limited by glycogen stores running out, but by having too much acid buildup.

    So, effectively, the body has five levels of cached energy:

    1. The mechanical energy stored in the elastic strain of the myosin.
    2. The chemical energy stored in ATP molecules. (Recharges myosin)
    3. The chemical energy stored in (phosph)ocreatine molecules. (Recharges ATP)
    4. The chemical energy stored in glycogen. (Recharges ATP and thus creatine.)
    5. The chemical energy stored in food and fat. (Used to recharge glycogen (food) and ATP (food or fat) and thus creatine.)

    ↩

肌酸是一种奇怪的类固醇激素或药物吗？

不。肌酸是一种营养素。 大多数杂食动物每天吃一两克肉。 您的身体每天还会合成一两克。 您需要肌酸来在细胞内输送能量。 这很正常，并不奇怪。

肌酸会增加睾丸激素吗？

不太可能。 这种担忧源自 2009 年一项针对 16 名男性橄榄球运动员的研究。1 但该研究被认为极其可疑。 至少有十二项其他研究都没有发现任何变化或生理上不相关的变化。 除此之外，肌酸会增加睾酮水平是难以置信的，因为我们知道肌酸的作用，而且它与激素无关。

肌酸会让你秃头吗？

不，或者更确切地说：

  1. 没有研究报道过这一点。
  2. 一项研究报告的结果恰恰相反。
  3. 没有任何机械理由认为这种情况会发生。
  4. 有充分的机械理由认为这种情况不会发生。

这些谣言都可以追溯到基于 2009 年同一项研究的猜测。 但这项研究与后来的研究相矛盾，而且无论如何都没有测量头发。 一切皆有可能，但据我所知，肌酸同样有可能促进头发生长。 如果你真的担心这个：你会停止吃肉吗？

肌酸安全吗？

大概。 国际运动营养学会表示：

对健康和患病人群（从婴儿到老年人）进行的短期和长期研究，剂量范围为 0.3 至 0.8 g/kg/天，持续长达 5 年，一致表明，肌酸补充剂不会造成不良健康风险，并可能提供许多健康和性能益处。

它已被广泛研究，尚未发现任何风险。 它的工作方式并不意味着任何风险。 每天补充几克并不会让你远远超出人们从正常食物中摄取的范围。

肌酸会让你变得更强壮吗？

是的。 补充剂拥有如此有力且一致的证据是非常罕见的。 一篇被广泛引用的评论称，短期补充可将最大功率/力量提高 5-15%。 这反过来可能会增加力量训练的长期收益。 肌酸还可将冲刺成绩提高 1-5%。 不过，像长跑这样的耐力运动似乎没有什么好处。

但肌酸如何让你变得更强壮呢？

在回答这个问题之前，我可以先咆哮一下肌肉是如何工作的吗？

…OK?

伟大的！ 以下是肌肉的工作原理：

  • 所有细胞内部都有一种名为 ATP 的分子漂浮，它们将其用作能量。
  • 肌肉细胞中含有称为肌球蛋白的蛋白质。
  • 当 ATP 碰到肌球蛋白时，肌球蛋白将 ATP 分解成 ADP。 这会释放能量，并被肌球蛋白以弹性应变的形式物理捕获。
  • 当被神经元触发时，肌球蛋白会释放机械能。
  • 当您决定移动手臂时，您的大脑会触发许多肌肉细胞，精心协调肌球蛋白抽搐以进行大规模运动。

现在，有一点对我们的故事至关重要：很少有能量以 ATP 形式存储。 您的身体含有约 100 克 ATP，相当于约 10,000 焦耳的能量。2 但您的身体在休息时会燃烧约 100 瓦。 因此，您储存的 ATP 只能维持大约 100 秒的生命。 如果你冲刺，你可以轻松燃烧约 3000 瓦，这将在约 3 秒内用完你存储的所有 ATP。

通过饮食的魔力，您总是会产生更多 ATP。 通常情况下，您的线粒体每秒将约 1 克 ADP 回收成 ATP，这与您维持生命所需的量相同。3 如果您开始跑步，您的身体可以将其提升至每秒约 10 克，尽管需要采取呼吸加快和心跳加快等技巧。4 但您的线粒体需要一两分钟才能真正开始运转。5

那么为什么我能够冲刺超过三秒呢？

因为肌酸充当额外的能量储存库，与 ATP 储存库耦合。 当您摄入或合成肌酸后，60% 会转化为磷酸肌酸。 这是通过一种酶来完成的，该酶捕获肌酸分子和 ATP 分子并在它们之间移动磷酸基团。 这会将肌酸“充电”为磷酸肌酸，并将 ATP“释放”为 ADP.6

但如果您的 ATP 水平下降——例如因为你正在逃离老虎，这些酶会反向运行，这意味着它们将磷酸肌酸“释放”为肌酸，并将 ADP“充电”回 ATP。 这种情况几乎立即发生，因此 ATP 和磷酸肌酸以相同的速率消耗。7

休息时，肌肉中磷酸肌酸的含量约为 ATP 的 3-4 倍。 因此，磷酸肌酸中储存的“额外”能量远大于 ATP 本身储存的“基础”能量。 这就是为什么你可以冲刺十秒而不是三秒。

补充肌酸会增加肌肉细胞中的肌酸水平吗？

是的。 典型的水平是：

  • 素食：100毫摩尔/公斤
  • 杂食动物：120毫摩尔/公斤
  • 补充肌酸的人：140毫摩尔/公斤

所以，一切似乎都加起来了。 如果补充肌酸，您的水平会增加约 16.67%，这意味着短期能量储存总量增加约 12.5%。8 这与肌酸试验中看到的力量增加 5-15% 一致。9 肌酸试验发现对耐力运动几乎没有什么好处，这似乎也是有道理的。 如果你没有突然爆发的活动，更大的短期能量储备不会真正帮助你。

但这一切是不是很奇怪？

嗯，我觉得很奇怪。 其他条件都一样，力量越大越好。 身体已经知道如何产生肌酸。 如果你可以提高肌酸水平并获得更多力量而没有任何副作用，那么进化不应该已经做到了这一点吗？ 阿尔吉农论点的某些变体表明，肌酸如此有效的事实应该是不可能的。

您可能认为较高的肌酸水平在某种程度上是不好的，这就是为什么进化没有使肌酸水平更高。 但这似乎是错误的。 肌酸水平自然会根据您所吃的食物而变化。 如果更高的水平是不好的，那么进化可能会让它们下降。 但事实并非如此。 它只是让它们有所不同。

通常，进化会让我们“更糟糕”地减少能量消耗，因为进化讨厌我们饿死。10但身体每天只消耗 1-2 卡路里来合成肌酸，肌肉细胞中更多的肌酸不会产生任何显着的代谢成本。

我认为无聊的解释是，对于我们的进化祖先来说，短期力量的适度增长并不是什么大问题。 我们是精疲力尽的猎人，而不是最多重复 1 次硬拉的猎人。11 此外，更多的肌酸会导致您的肌肉细胞吸收一些额外的水，这会稍微增加长跑的能量消耗。12 因此，如果您碰巧从肉类中获得了额外的肌酸，那就太好了。 如果没有，那也没关系。 在肌酸根据饮食波动的范围内，我怀疑肌酸水平对生殖成功没有太大影响。

尽管如此，我们必须承认肌酸是不寻常的。 我希望我们能告诉我们的身体，“嘿，我们可以获得无限量的食物。 别再担心节省能量，专注于变得更出色。” 但我们几乎没有办法做到这一点。 据我所知，已被证明可以增加力量的正常营养素清单是：蛋白质、肌酸、β-丙氨酸，最后。

所以肌酸很特别。 肌酸可以让你变得更强壮。 它是否也让你变得更聪明一点？

肌酸被大脑使用吗？

是的。 身体的大部分部位都不含有大量的肌酸。 但大脑、肌肉、心脏和睾丸确实如此。 神经元用它来玩肌肉与 ATP 和磷酸盐基团等相同的游戏。

大脑中有多少肌酸？

也许是肌肉的一半。 这里感兴趣的数字是磷酸肌酸与 ATP 的比率，表明磷酸肌酸增加局部能量储存的程度。 我们在上面看到肌肉的比例是 3 比 4。 在大脑中，数字有点粗略，但比例似乎更像是 1.5 到 2.13

但为什么？ 为什么大脑会使用肌酸？

好问题！ 大脑不像肌肉那样消耗能量。 是的，大脑消耗了约 20% 的卡路里，尽管大脑只占体重的约 2%。 但大脑的不同寻常之处在于，它需要所有能量来完成基本的家务管理，并且不会随着使用而增加。 与普遍的误解相反，努力思考并不会燃烧更多的卡路里。 （示范：开始认真思考，观察你的心率是否增加。）

因此肌肉使用肌酸进行冲刺。 但大脑没有冲刺。 那么大脑到底用肌酸做什么呢？

最常见的理论似乎是这样的：实际上，肌肉不仅仅使用肌酸作为额外的能量储存库。 他们还用它在细胞内传递能量。 您会看到，肌酸在细胞内的扩散速度比 ATP 快。 因此，即使进行耐力运动，肌酸仍然被使用：线粒体附近的酶使用 ATP 将肌酸“充电”为磷酸肌酸，肌球蛋白附近的酶使用磷酸肌酸将 ADP“充电”回 ATP。 尽管肌酸的净变化为零，但它有助于将能量从线粒体“穿梭”到肌球蛋白。

根据这个理论，神经元和肌肉细胞的共同之处在于，当它们被触发时，细胞的某些部分会局部使用大量能量。 因此，即使您的大脑没有“冲刺”，它仍然会使用肌酸来避免局部能量不足。

还有实验证据表明肌酸对大脑很重要。 我们培育出了经过基因改造的小鼠，其大脑缺乏将肌酸转化为磷酸肌酸或从磷酸肌酸转化为肌酸所需的酶。 他们的空间学习能力非常有限，大脑也较小。

有些人天生就患有肌酸缺乏症。 在某些变体中，人们无法合成肌酸。 这会导致全身肌酸水平降低，其中包括 95% 的肌酸所在的骨骼肌。 然而，主要症状与大脑有关，即智力障碍。 肌肉无力和癫痫发作也很常见。 其他人患有肌酸转运蛋白缺乏症，这意味着肌酸无法穿过血脑屏障。 这只会导致大脑中的水平降低。 这再次导致智力障碍，并且常常导致肌肉无力或癫痫发作。 （尽管肌肉细胞本身具有正常的肌酸水平，但肌肉无力仍然存在。）14

所以不知何故，肌酸对大脑非常重要。

补充肌酸会增加大脑中的肌酸水平吗？

有可能，尽管可能比肌肉少。

肌酸绝对可以穿过血脑屏障。 然而，帮助其穿过的蛋白质并不丰富，并且有一些建议表明，随着长期肌酸的消耗，它的调节会下调。 大脑本身可以合成一些肌酸，而长期食用也可能会下调肌酸的含量。

当然，你可以给人们肌酸，看看他们的大脑会发生什么变化。 类似的研究大约有十几个。 大多数报告增加了 3% 到 10%，但也有少数报告没有变化。 然而，由于大脑很难进入，这些研究依赖于磁共振波谱学，一些人认为这些测量结果不可靠。

对于无法合成肌酸的人来说，口服补充剂似乎可以使大脑中的肌酸水平正常化。 （通常仍然存在一些认知障碍。 一名患者在三周大时被诊断并开始补充，没有智力障碍。） 因此，在某些情况下，补充可以提高大脑水平。

我最好的猜测是，补充剂通常确实会增加大脑中的水平，并且增加 3% 到 10% 是合理的。 但证据并不是特别有力。

为什么人们对具有认知益处的肌酸感兴趣？

因为 Rae 等人。 （2003）。 他们带了 45 名健康的素食主义者或澳大利亚纯素大学学生。 他们进行了一项交叉试验，其中一半的人每天摄入 5 克肌酸，持续六周，然后是六周的清除期，然后另一半人摄入肌酸。 他们的结果令人惊叹，对 Raven 矩阵 (RAPM) 和后向数字跨度 (BDS) 有了巨大的改进：

在他们的分析中，肌酸使 BDS 增加了 1.19 个标准差，使 RAPM 增加了 1.76 个标准差。 如果我们将这些数字转换为智商点（其中 1 个标准差←> 15 智商点），则意味着智商分别增加 17.85 点和 26.4 点。 在这两种情况下，结果均非常显着 (p < 0.0001)。

这能复制吗？

不。在那篇论文之后，各个小组尝试了类似的实验，但没有人发现如此大或具有统计显着性的效果。 经过二十年的不确定结果后，Sandkühler 等人。 （2023）着手给出最终的复制品。 在我看来，这是有史以来关于肌酸认知益处的最高质量的随机对照试验。 15 他们很大程度上借鉴了 Rae 等人的实验设计，尽管他们在德国进行了实验，使用了 123 人的更大样本，使用了一半非素食者，并且放弃了洗脱期。 以下是他们的主要结果：

（T1 显示基线测试结果。 T2 显示服用肌酸或安慰剂六周后的结果。 T3 显示了另外六周后的结果，其中安慰剂组交叉到肌酸组，反之亦然。）

总的来说，随着时间的推移，每个人都变得更好，可能是通过练习。 从向后的数字跨度来看，在前六周内，服用安慰剂的组实际上比服用肌酸的组改善得稍快。 但当这些组在服用安慰剂和肌酸之间切换时，该组（以前是安慰剂，现在是肌酸）改善得更快。 只要看一下图表，就会发现这有一些好处。 在瑞文矩阵上，同样的事情发生了，但幅度大大降低了。

他们拟合了一个统计模型，并报告向后数字跨度的效应大小为 0.17 个标准差（约 2.5 个 IQ 点，在统计上不显着），Raven 矩阵的效应大小为 0.09 个标准差（约 1 个 IQ 点，甚至不显着）。 他们没有发现素食者有任何额外的好处，甚至没有一个不显着的好处。

据我所知，这种差异从未得到令人信服的解释。 Rae 等人 2003 年的实验似乎做得很好。 结果太大，无法用 p-hacking 来解释，而且统计意义太大，无法用随机噪声来解释。 也许出于某种原因，Rae 等人的队列基线肌酸水平较低？ 这很奇怪。 但历史表明，当令人兴奋的结果伴随着令人失望的复制时，我们应该押注于令人失望的复制。

其他所有随机对照试验又如何呢？ 这难道不需要荟萃分析吗？

原则上是的。 问题是，大多数研究没有报告良好荟萃分析所需的数据。 他们做了一些实验，让一半的人服用肌酸，另一半则服用安慰剂，并测量这些群体在认知测试中的表现。 然后他们拟合一些统计模型并报告 p 值或其他什么。 但他们从未真正公布原始平均值和标准差。 16

对我们来说幸运的是，徐等人。 （2024）联系了所有这些试验的作者并获取了他们的原始数据。 根据他们的荟萃分析，肌酸具有以下作用。

领域 效应大小（标准差）
整体认知功能 +0.34
执行职能 +0.32
注意力 +0.22
记忆 +0.31
处理速度 +0.01

对我们来说不幸的是，那张纸很糟糕。 他们声称其中一些结果具有统计显着性，但 2026 年的评论指出，他们犯了一个错误，相当于对多项研究的相同数据进行了重复计算。 17 因此，我没有显示他们的（不正确的）置信区间。 如果计算正确，我怀疑所有结果都不会具有统计显着性。 从技术上讲，上述点估计也是错误的，尽管该错误不应系统性地使它们偏向任何一个方向。

总的来说，我必须告诉你，我真的不相信这篇论文。 它非常草率，缺少大量细节。 但据我所知，没有人收集过进行良好荟萃分析所需的数据。 所以我认为这些数字是我们拥有的最好的总结。

那么我们可以信任谁呢？

我会告诉你我信任谁：欧洲食品和安全局（EFSA）。 2024 年，一家销售肌酸的公司向欧盟申请允许宣传认知益处。 这导致 EFSA 发布了肌酸和认知功能的改善：根据法规 (EC) No 1924/2006 第 13(5) 条对健康声明进行评估。

以下是他们不得不说的内容（为了便于阅读，我删除了参考文献）：

小组认为，总体而言，10 项人类干预研究 [...] 没有显示出肌酸补充剂对认知功能的一致影响。 专家小组指出，一些研究报告了肌酸对工作记忆的急性影响[…] 在较低肌酸剂量下没有观察到[…] 或持续摄入肌酸。 专家小组还指出，肌酸的作用 [...] 一项研究报告的证据是一个孤立的发现，其中没有观察到肌酸补充剂对其他认知领域的影响，包括记忆的不同方面（情景记忆、短期记忆、视觉记忆）、语言流畅性、注意力、警觉性、处理速度、精神运动速度、执行功能和一般认知能力/灵活性和流体智力。 最后，专家组指出，在患病个体中进行的三项干预研究并不支持补充肌酸对认知的影响。

我认为我们应该考虑这一点。 我什至可以说，这份文件可能代表了我们的文明为了解肌酸是否具有认知益处而做出的最大努力。

但我们需要记住 ESFA 的作用。 他们询问肌酸是否已被证明具有认知益处，因为他们正在决定宣传认知益处是否应该合法。 他们说不，我相信他们。 但这并不意味着没有认知方面的好处。

还有其他 RCT 综述吗？

是的。 以下是我能找到的所有最近评论，其中每条评论都有一些代表性的引述：

审查 引号
阿夫杰里诺斯等人。 (2018) “有证据表明短期记忆和智力/推理可以通过肌酸给药得到改善。”“年轻人的认知任务表现保持不变。”“素食者在记忆任务中比肉食者反应更好”
多兰等人。 (2019) “血脑屏障是肌酸循环的障碍”“可能会提高某些认知任务的表现，特别是在压力条件下（例如精神疲劳、力竭运动）。”
罗斯切尔等人。 (2021) “补充肌酸有可能改善认知处理，特别是在以大脑肌酸缺陷为特征的情况下”“需要同时评估大脑肌酸水平和认知功能的补充研究”
普罗科皮迪斯等人。 (2023) “校正后，我们的总体分析表明，一水肌酸不会改善整体记忆表现（标准化平均差 0.19；95% 置信区间，–0.07，0.46）”
徐等人。 (2024) “补充肌酸对记忆力和注意力时间有显着的积极影响，并显着提高处理速度。 然而，整体认知功能或执行功能没有发现显着改善。”
麦克莫里斯等人。 (2024) “补充肌酸对处于无压力情况下的年轻健康参与者没有显着影响。 此外，审查显示压力群体的结果好坏参半。”“素食主义者没有摄入足够的[…] 肌酸以确保维持最佳认知输出所需的水平。”“对[证据]的仔细检查表明，补充可能比迄今为止的研究提供的更积极的结果。”
英国 NHCC (2024) “每天摄入 ≤3g 肌酸与认知功能改善之间尚未建立因果关系。”

平均而言，随机对照试验确实发现了微小的积极作用，但不是统计上显着的积极作用。 正如我经常指出的那样，如果真正的影响是积极的但很小，这正是我们所期望的。 但这也完全有可能是由于随机机会、p-hacking 或出版偏见造成的。 格温联系了一位作者，发现确实存在发表偏见。

总体而言，我认为随机对照试验提供的证据非常薄弱，表明对健康成年人有微小的益处。 （可能有 0.1 到 0.3 个标准差，具体取决于测量方法。） 我还认为，它们提供了适度的证据，证明对健康成年人有更大的影响（例如，0.5 个标准差以上），并提供了微弱的证据，证明对因某种方式“压力”而可能减少肌酸的成年人有微小的好处，例如年龄较大、素食主义者或身体疲惫。

您能总结一下支持肌酸使您变得更聪明的证据吗？

我很乐意这样做：

  • 肌酸很特别。 很少有营养素能真正让你变得更强壮，但肌酸却可以。
  • 除了肌肉之外，身体的其他部分很少使用大量的肌酸，但大脑却会。
  • 肌酸可以穿过血脑屏障。
  • 肌酸对于大脑的正常运作至关重要。
  • 补充肌酸可能会增加大脑中的肌酸水平，至少会增加一点。
  • 一些随机对照试验表明其具有认知益处。

您能总结一下反对肌酸使您变得更聪明的证据吗？

Yes:

  • 我们并不完全了解大脑如何使用肌酸。 目前还没有明确的机制来解释为什么补充肌酸会让你变得更聪明。
  • 大脑如何使用肌酸的最佳类比是肌肉如何使用肌酸进行耐力锻炼。 但肌酸对于耐力运动几乎没有什么好处。
  • 目前尚未确定补充肌酸会在多大程度上（或是否）增加大脑中的肌酸水平。
  • 随机对照试验表明其益处非常小，大约为 1 到 3 IQ 点。
  • 随机对照试验没有统计学意义。

肌酸会让你更聪明吗？

我不知道。 也许有一点。

你可以提出这样的论点：肌酸对大脑至关重要（以某种方式），因此保持高水平是最安全的，以防万一。 但我不确定我买那个。 肌酸对大脑至关重要，但有一些迹象表明进化知道这一点，因此对大脑中肌酸水平的调节比对肌肉中的肌酸水平更严格。

我可能会相信素食者或严格素食者的观点。 但几乎没有实验证据表明这些群体有额外的认知益处，甚至有一些证据表明素食者的大脑肌酸水平可能不会低得多，尽管摄入量大大降低。

如果肌酸有帮助，那么可能带来的好处可能很小。 假设您认为肌酸有 50% 的机会使 IQ 提高 1 分，而有 50% 的机会它没有用。 为了预期智商提高 0.5 分，每天服用 5 克肌酸真的值得吗？ 我不知道。
